lint.command: cd /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control && bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

nonBlockingIssues.description: `CompanyContextFilter.SUPER_ADMIN_TENANT_BUSINESS_PREFIXES` still contains `/api/v1/support`, leaving stale retired-host knowledge in the auth filter even though the shared support controller surface is gone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When retiring an endpoint family, explicitly audit shared auth/filter path registries and prefix lists (for example `CompanyContextFilter.SUPER_ADMIN_TENANT_BUSINESS_PREFIXES`) so retired hosts are removed from global routing assumptions, not only from controllers/OpenAPI.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `support-host-split-hard-cut` review passed but found stale `/api/v1/support` knowledge left in `CompanyContextFilter` after the shared support surface was retired.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether `gate-release` and `gate-reconciliation` are required for every feature handoff or only for final PR/packet readiness, and document the expected proof when they are intentionally deferred.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `support-host-split-hard-cut` handoff explicitly skipped `gate-release` and `gate-reconciliation` because a follow-up feature was still pending, while the skill text can be read as requiring them before a feature handoff.
